description: A read-modify-write under default read-committed isolation loses one transaction's update when two run concurrently.
body: Failure mode: two transactions read the same row (e.g. balance), each computes a new value in app code, and both write; under read-committed with no row lock the second overwrites the first, silently losing an update. Detection signals: totals drift under concurrency; the pattern is SELECT then UPDATE in app code with no FOR UPDATE; bugs reproduce only under load. Do not assume read-committed serializes read-modify-write. Safer fallback: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to lock the row, do the update atomically in SQL, or raise to serializable and retry on serialization failures, with a concurrency test proving correctness.
